title: Lens nigricans
intro: Lens nigricans, also known as Black-eyed pea or Black-eyed bean, is a type of legume belonging to the Fabaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include lowering cholesterol levels, reducing inflammation, and aiding in weight management due to its high fiber and protein content. The bioactive constituents of Lens nigricans include isoflavones, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its potential health benefits. In traditional herbal medicine, Lens nigricans is often used to make teas, soups, and dried powders, as well as being used as an ingredient in various supplements and functional foods.
